name: Col du Tourmalet Summit
roadSegment: D918 Col du Tourmalet - Souvenir Jacques Goddet summit
summary: Iconic HC summit candidate with maximum race drama, but access from Arreau is not feasible to assume until official closure and parking rules are published.
whyItMatters: The Tour schedule makes the Tourmalet the HC Souvenir Jacques Goddet point, and the viewing guide strongly favors climbs for slower rider passage when logistics are solved.

access.estimated_route_from_base: From Arreau, likely via D918/Col d'Aspin/Sainte-Marie-de-Campan/La Mongie side in normal road conditions; race-day vehicle feasibility unknown.
access.parking_transit_possibilities: Unknown. Do not assume summit parking, roadside parking, camper parking, or shuttle access.
access.walk_bike_approach_notes: Walking/cycling from lower access points may be required after road closure, but official allowance and distance are unknown.
access.road_closure_notes: Unknown for 2026 Tour stage; mountain roads can close far earlier than rider passage.

pros: Legendary Tour de France HC climb and Souvenir Jacques Goddet summit.
Slow rider speeds and dense mountain atmosphere.
Official schedule point gives clear timing for later passage-time work.
cons: Highest logistical risk of all candidates.
Crowding, weather exposure, limited toilets/water, and slow descent/exit are likely problems.
Could be inaccessible by car from Arreau once closures begin.

logistics.parking: Unknown; summit/roadside parking cannot be assumed.
logistics.transit: Unknown; no official Tourmalet race-day shuttle source found.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Needs Hautes-Pyrenees prefecture/department road order and Tour spectator plan.

crowdRisk: very high: iconic HC summit, limited space, and mountain-pass bottlenecks.
bathroomFoodProximity: Unknown and likely limited. Any summit restaurants/toilets must be verified as open and reachable after barriers.
escapeRoute: Expect a delayed exit. Descend only after police/organizer clearance; likely bottleneck either toward La Mongie/Sainte-Marie-de-Campan or toward Bareges.
safetyAssessment: Avoid inside bends, narrow shoulders, drop-offs, and standing where team cars/motos need room. Bring water, layers, food, and offline maps.

timing.best_arrival_window: Morning at minimum; consider night-before/very early arrival only if official access and parking allow it.
timing.race_day_risk: Very high until closure, parking, and pedestrian/cyclist access notices are published.

sources.title: Tour de France 2026 Stage 6 official page
sources.note: Supports Stage 6 route, timing, Col du Tourmalet schedule point, HC category, 17.1 km climb at 7.3%, and Souvenir Jacques Goddet status.

sources.title: TDF trip data official route note
sources.note: Official ASO ArcGIS layer referenced by the app for Stage 6 route geometry; coordinate matches the app's selected Tourmalet point.

sources.title: Col du Tourmalet background
sources.note: Supports elevation, D918 pass context, eastern/western access towns, winter closure pattern, and Tourmalet's Tour history; not a 2026 closure source.

open_questions: What time will D918 and approach roads close to vehicles, cyclists, and pedestrians?
Is any official parking/shuttle/camper plan published for the Tourmalet stage?
Are summit toilets, food, water, and medical points available to general spectators?
